document_metadata.title: UK protests latest: Keir Starmer calls out 'far-right thuggery' on streets after Rotherham hotel attacked
document_metadata.first_words: ### Another day of chaos across Englandpublished at 20:45 BST 4 August 2024 Jamie Whitehead Live reporter We're bringing our coverage of the violence seen in several towns around England to a close now. A riot in Rotherham was followed by violent scenes in Middlesbrough, with chanting between rival groups and one arrest. If you want more on this story: - Starmer condemns "far right thuggery" after more violence - Southport has 'mixed emotions' as disorder follows attack - Opposing protest groups separated by police in Weymouth - And in Hull, the community shows trouble "does not represent" the city Thank you for joining us today, the coverage was bought to you by Sophie Abdullah, Seher Asaf, Ruth Comerford, Sean Seddon, Zahra Fatima, Lucy Clarke-Billings, Tiffany Wertheimer, George Bowden, Sam Horti, Tom McArthur and me.

chunks.content: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has condemned what he has called “far-right thuggery“ in the form of violent demonstrations. Speaking to reporters at Downing Street just now, the prime minister said those involved will feel the “full force of the law“. “You will regret taking part in this,“ he told those involved in the unrest. “There is no justification for taking this action.“ He said people in this country “have a right to be safe“ and yet we've seen “Muslim communities targeted... Nazi salutes in the street“. “I won't shy away from calling it what it is - far-right thuggery,“ Starmer said.
chunks.content_original: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has condemned what he has called “far-right thuggery“ in the form of violent demonstrations. Speaking to reporters at Downing Street just now, the prime minister said those involved will feel the “full force of the law“. “You will regret taking part in this,“ he told those involved in the unrest. “There is no justification for taking this action.“ He said people in this country “have a right to be safe“ and yet we've seen “Muslim communities targeted... Nazi salutes in the street“. “I won't shy away from calling it what it is - far-right thuggery,“ Starmer said.

chunks.content: Home Secretary Yvette Cooper has posted on X about the scenes in Rotherham. “The criminal, violent attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham is utterly appalling,“ she says. “Deliberately setting fire to a building with people known to be inside. South Yorkshire Police have full Government support for the strongest action against those responsible.“
chunks.content_original: Home Secretary Yvette Cooper has posted on X about the scenes in Rotherham. “The criminal, violent attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham is utterly appalling,“ she says. “Deliberately setting fire to a building with people known to be inside. South Yorkshire Police have full Government support for the strongest action against those responsible.“

chunks.content: Starmer vowed to do “whatever it takes to bring these thugs to justice“ as he addressed the nation following ongoing unrest across parts of the country. He said there will be arrests, there will be charges and there will be convictions, “whatever the apparent cause or motivation“. “This is not a protest, it is organised, violent thuggery and it has no place on our streets or online,“ he said. He specifically condemned an attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham this afternoon. The PM said those participating in violence, including those “whipping up this action online“, would regret it.
chunks.content_original: Starmer vowed to do “whatever it takes to bring these thugs to justice“ as he addressed the nation following ongoing unrest across parts of the country. He said there will be arrests, there will be charges and there will be convictions, “whatever the apparent cause or motivation“. “This is not a protest, it is organised, violent thuggery and it has no place on our streets or online,“ he said. He specifically condemned an attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham this afternoon. The PM said those participating in violence, including those “whipping up this action online“, would regret it.

chunks.content: Since Saturday night, police have made 147 arrests across the country, and that number is expected to rise, BJ Harrington of the National Police Chiefs’ Council has said in a statement. He blamed disinformation for driving the “appalling violence“, adding that “often posts are being shared and amplified by high profile accounts“. He also had a warning for those involved in the unrest: “Intelligence teams, detectives and neighbourhood officers are working round the clock to identify and apprehend those involved and make no mistake, if you haven’t had a knock on the door yet, your time will come.“
chunks.content_original: Since Saturday night, police have made 147 arrests across the country, and that number is expected to rise, BJ Harrington of the National Police Chiefs’ Council has said in a statement. He blamed disinformation for driving the “appalling violence“, adding that “often posts are being shared and amplified by high profile accounts“. He also had a warning for those involved in the unrest: “Intelligence teams, detectives and neighbourhood officers are working round the clock to identify and apprehend those involved and make no mistake, if you haven’t had a knock on the door yet, your time will come.“

chunks.content: Faith leaders across Merseyside have said “too many people have sought to use the [Southport] tragedy to create division and hate“. Leaders from the Catholic, Muslim, Jewish, Sikh and Hindu communities, among others, issued a joint statement this afternoon. “Division can destroy the very relationships and environment that we depend upon every day of our lives and there is no place for hate in our communities,“ it read. The statement called for “community spirit“ and urged people to remember that “there is far more that unites than divides us“. The Home Office has just announced a new security scheme for mosques. It says mosques “at risk of violent disorder“ will now be able to request “rapid response“ personnel. “The police, local authorities and mosques can ask for rapid security to be deployed, protecting communities and allowing for a return to worship as quickly as possible,“ a Home Office statement says. The new security measures will “build on“ the government's existing Protective Security for Mosques Scheme, which already funds security at mosques and Muslim schools. Home Secretary Yvette Cooper said: “As a nation we will not tolerate criminal behaviour, dangerous extremism, and racist attacks that go against everything our country stands for.” Earlier, PM Keir Starmer called unrest in UK streets “far-right thuggery“, and said that Muslim communities had been targeted.

chunks.content: Starmer described the scenes in Rotherham as “marauding gangs intent on law-breaking and nothing else“ and said they left “residents and staff in absolute fear“. To those that feel targeted, he said: “I know how frightening it must be.“ He promised that the “violent mob do not represent our country“ and said: “If you target people because of the colour of their skin or faith, that is far-right and I’m prepared to say so.“ “Our police deserve our support as they tackle any and all violent disorder that flares up,“ he added. The PM said he had held meetings throughout the weekend to make sure the “necessary support“ is in place.

chunks.content: There have been several fast-moving developments today - here's the latest: In Rotherham, hundreds of anti-immigration protesters surrounded the Holiday Inn Express hotel, where some people lit a fire, smashed windows and doors and managed to get inside the building where asylum seekers were staying. Riot police surrounded the hotel and pushed crowds back, but the scene there remains tense. Crowds in Middlesbrough set fire to an overturned car and pushed burning wheelie bins at police. A protest in Bolton started off peacefully, but then became heated when two groups clashed. One, most with faces covered by face masks or balaclavas - charged towards town hall shouting “Allahu Akbar“ - or God is greatest. They clashed with a separate group waving England flags. There were heated scenes in Bolton where two groups of protesters clashed. There have also been protests in Hull and Weymouth. PM Keir Starmer has condemned the “far-right thuggery“ and vowed to do “whatever it takes to bring these thugs to justice“. The Home Office says that mosques will be offered greater protection with new “emergency security“ measures that can be rapidly deployed where needed. And 147 arrests have been made across the country since Saturday night, and police are expecting that number to rise. Stay with us, we'll continue to bring you all the latest updates.

chunks.content: We've seen crowds gather today in Rotherham, Bolton, Hull, Southport, Middlesbrough, and elsewhere, with varying levels of unrest. In Middlesbrough, the BBC team has witnessed demonstrators carry a banner referring to the Southport knife attacks, before throwing cans and bottles at police officers and shops with the shutters down. Youths wearing masks have attacked cars, with one overturned and set alight. Cars and homes in side streets around the town centre have also been targeted, their windows smashed. Wheelie bins have also been set alight. The local police force, Cleveland Police, say they've made nine arrests so far. People are being advised to stay away from the area.
chunks.content_original: We've seen crowds gather today in Rotherham, Bolton, Hull, Southport, Middlesbrough, and elsewhere, with varying levels of unrest. In Middlesbrough, the BBC team has witnessed demonstrators carry a banner referring to the Southport knife attacks, before throwing cans and bottles at police officers and shops with the shutters down. Youths wearing masks have attacked cars, with one overturned and set alight. Cars and homes in side streets around the town centre have also been targeted, their windows smashed. Wheelie bins have also been set alight. The local police force, Cleveland Police, say they've made nine arrests so far. People are being advised to stay away from the area.

chunks.content: South Yorkshire Police (SYP) says at least 10 officers were injured in the “deplorable acts of violence“ at the Holiday Inn Express Hotel in Rotherham earlier today. One officer was left unconscious after a head injury, SYP says, with other officers suffering suspected broken bones, including a suspected fractured elbow. There have so far been no reports of any hotel staff or residents being injured in the disorder. “We absolutely condemn the scenes of disorder we witnessed, with a total of around 700 people in attendance in Manvers Way earlier today,“ a statement says. “A large bin close to a window of the hotel was also set alight causing a small fire which was later extinguished, with missiles, including glass bottles and beer cans, thrown at our officers.“

One officer was left unconscious after a head injury, SYP says, with other officers suffering suspected broken bones, including a suspected fractured elbow. There have so far been no reports of any hotel staff or residents being injured in the disorder. “We absolutely condemn the scenes of disorder we witnessed, with a total of around 700 people in attendance in Manvers Way earlier today,“ a statement says. “A large bin close to a window of the hotel was also set alight causing a small fire which was later extinguished, with missiles, including glass bottles and beer cans, thrown at our officers.“

chunks.content: Keir Starmer has sounded tough on the violent disorder for days - today branding it “far-right thuggery“. His warning to those taking part that they will regret it extended not just to those committing criminal acts on the streets, but also those stirring it up online. Mosques have been offered emergency security, extra prosecutors have been brought in, and ministerial meetings have been held over the weekend. But still the violence continues, and it remains to be seen whether tough language will bring it to an end. The government believes that once people see arrests, charges and convictions becoming a reality, they will think twice about taking part. For communities and individuals who feel at risk, it has already been going on too long.

Mosques have been offered emergency security, extra prosecutors have been brought in, and ministerial meetings have been held over the weekend. But still the violence continues, and it remains to be seen whether tough language will bring it to an end. The government believes that once people see arrests, charges and convictions becoming a reality, they will think twice about taking part. For communities and individuals who feel at risk, it has already been going on too long.

chunks.content: Dorset Police have built a physical wall using themselves and about five riot vans to stop the two protest groups meeting on the road and the esplanade. The anti-immigration group is slowly moving towards Stand up to Racism Dorset, which the police are trying to stop. They’ve called in more resources and in the last 10 minutes, more police vans have turned up. Traffic is at a standstill in the town centre with buses and cars trying to leave the area, but they are stuck waiting at the King’s Statue.

chunks.content: About 400 protestors from opposing sides are gathered on Weymouth seafront chanting at each other. On one side, the crowd is chanting “Weymouth and Portland welcome refugees“ with placards saying “Nazis go home“. The other group are chanting “Tommy Robinson“, waving Union Jack flags and holding signs that read “I [love] my country“. Officers have moved people off the beach because some anti-immigration protesters pushed through the police line and ran up the seafront. Protesters from opposing sides are gradually getting closer to each other.

chunks.content: Turning to Scotland, where the First Minister John Swinney has warned against the “unhelpful speculation“ surrounding the identity of a man who allegedly stabbed a 21-year-old woman in the central Scottish city of Stirling on Saturday. She was treated in hospital for “non-life threatening“ injuries, and a 29-year old man has been arrested. However, social media rumours, promoted by EDL founder Tommy Robinson, suggested the attacker was Muslim. Police Scotland have said these claims are false, and in quite an unusual move for the force, have confirmed the man arrested is white and from the local area. Swinney says the incident is “isolated“, but his predecessor, Humza Yousaf is now urging PM Keir Starmer to call in the army to try and quell some of the violence we're seeing in places like Rotherham. He wrote on X: “How much worse does it have to get before the army is sent in Keir Starmer? The police clearly do not have a handle on this situation“.

chunks.content: There’s been tensions over immigration here in Weymouth since April last year, when it was announced that the Bibby Stockholm would be housing asylum seekers waiting for their applications to be processed. That caused a divide in the community and two groups formed - but both opposed the barge that is now berthed at Portland Port. One saying they didn’t feel safe with around 500 foreign men aged 18 - 65 being brought in to the community. The other group felt it was inhumane to house people on a barge that they say is similar to a prison. It’s been protest after protest here since summer last year with both groups clashing, so today is nothing different for the community of Weymouth and Portland.

chunks.content: Earlier today around 300 people gathered to guard the Jamia Masjid al-Madinah mosque in Middlesbrough, as anti-immigration demonstrations marched through the town. At least one car has been set alight in the Teesside town and burning wheelie bins have been pushed toward police officers. “We're going to be paying for this, and for what?“ one resident told PA News Agency. “This has nothing to do with three little girls that have lost their lives. They've used it to cause utter mayhem on our streets“. Cleveland Police say 14 arrests have been made so far.

to Scotland, where the First Minister John Swinney has warned against the \"unhelpful speculation\" surrounding the identity of a man who allegedly stabbed a 21-year-old woman in the central Scottish city of Stirling on Saturday. She was treated in hospital for \"non-life threatening\" injuries, and a 29-year old man has been arrested. However, social media rumours, promoted by EDL founder Tommy Robinson, suggested the attacker was Muslim. Police Scotland have said these claims are false, and in quite an unusual move for the force, have confirmed the man arrested is white and from the local area. Swinney says the incident is \"isolated\", but his predecessor, Humza Yousaf is now urging PM Keir Starmer to call in the army to try and quell some of the violence we're seeing in places like Rotherham. He wrote on X: \"How much worse does it have to get before the army is sent in Keir Starmer? The police clearly do not have a handle on this situation\"."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:aad2f8d7-c598-4a65-b9f8-fe15b59baf83","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Today is nothing different for Weymouth and Portland","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/554e4e87-3c28-472d-bf4a-60196f2ebcf0.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T18:50:36.729Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T18:56:05.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"There’s been tensions over\nimmigration here in Weymouth since April last year, when it was announced that\nthe Bibby Stockholm would be housing asylum seekers waiting for their\napplications to be processed. That caused a divide in the community and two groups\nformed - but both opposed the barge that\nis now berthed at Portland Port. One saying they didn’t feel safe with around 500\nforeign men aged 18 - 65 being brought in to the community. The other group felt it was inhumane to house people on\na barge that they say is similar to a prison. It’s been protest after protest here since summer last\nyear with both groups clashing, so today is nothing different for the community\nof Weymouth and Portland. "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:3efc9dcf-799f-4690-8e0d-e67ab6de2e37","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Crowds gather to protect Middlesbrough mosque","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/6a5100f2-9d13-43d3-8e03-66285fbc7f40.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T18:34:47.433Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T19:33:56.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Earlier today around 300 people gathered to guard the\nJamia Masjid al-Madinah mosque in Middlesbrough, as anti-immigration demonstrations\nmarched through the town. At\nleast one car has been set alight in the Teesside town and burning wheelie bins\nhave been pushed toward police officers. \"We're going to be paying for this, and for\nwhat?\" one resident told PA News Agency. \"This has nothing to do with three little\ngirls that have lost their lives. They've used it to cause utter mayhem on our\nstreets\". Cleveland Police say 14 arrests have been\nmade so far. "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:d0aae887-55c8-44c1-8a6b-978530fcaea9","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Protesters contained in Weymouth","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T18:23:50.363Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T18:23:50.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Police have\ncontained protesters here on Weymouth esplanade. Around 30\npolice from Dorset and the neighbouring counties of Devon and Cornwall are\nmanaging the groups. Both sides\nare still chanting with one shouting “save our streets” and the other “shame on\nyou” Traffic is\nnow moving and the buses and cars that were at a standstill have moved on with\nthe road fully open around the King’s Statue.\nPolice riot vans are still surrounding the pedestrianised sea front blocking in\nthe protestors, away from members of the public."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:26e2e5ca-f7b3-4529-9b8e-f06827ed9681","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Ten police officers injured in Rotherham hotel violence","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/f5dcbe90-1e17-4ba0-8dec-94305953586f.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T18:12:01.171Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T18:14:53.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"South Yorkshire Police (SYP) says at least 10 officers were injured in the \"deplorable acts of violence\" at the Holiday Inn Express Hotel in Rotherham earlier today. One officer was left unconscious after a head injury, SYP says, with other officers suffering suspected broken bones, including a suspected fractured elbow. There have so far been no reports of any hotel staff or residents being injured in the disorder. \"We absolutely condemn the scenes of disorder we witnessed, with a total of around 700 people in attendance in Manvers Way earlier today,\" a statement says.  \"A large bin close to a window of the hotel was also set alight causing a small fire which was later extinguished, with missiles, including glass bottles and beer cans, thrown at our officers.\" "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:f5be4b0f-1658-43b3-bf31-15dbdcf94a05","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Will Starmer's tough talk be enough to quell the unrest?","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T17:47:12.285Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T17:47:11.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Keir Starmer has sounded tough on the violent\ndisorder for days - today branding it \"far-right thuggery\". His warning to those taking part that they will\nregret it extended not just to those committing criminal acts on the streets,\nbut also those stirring it up online. Mosques have been offered emergency security, extra\nprosecutors have been brought in, and ministerial meetings have been held over\nthe weekend. But still the violence continues, and it remains to\nbe seen whether tough language will bring it to an end. The government believes that once people see\narrests, charges and convictions becoming a reality, they will think twice\nabout taking part. For\ncommunities and individuals who feel at risk, it has already been going on too\nlong."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:d0c87736-3ff3-4072-9de5-779b7c8dd7f0","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"More police arrive in Weymouth","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2791ca4a-db67-40bc-86d6-908aad6261b0.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T17:27:18.223Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T17:35:18.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Dorset Police have built a physical wall using\nthemselves and about five riot vans to stop the two protest groups meeting on\nthe road and the esplanade. The anti-immigration group is slowly moving towards\nStand up to Racism Dorset, which the police are trying to stop.  They’ve called in more resources and in the last 10\nminutes, more police vans have turned up. Traffic\nis at a standstill in the town centre with buses and cars trying to leave the\narea, but they are stuck waiting at the King’s Statue."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:f9607b97-2f2e-4cec-9fc1-607461858b87","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Protesters face off in Weymouth","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/fc470400-f100-44df-840c-a190d6b5a6eb.png.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T17:13:43.866Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T17:13:43.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"About 400 protestors from opposing sides are\ngathered on Weymouth seafront chanting at each other. On one side, the crowd is chanting \"Weymouth\nand Portland welcome refugees\" with placards saying \"Nazis go\nhome\". The other group are chanting \"Tommy\nRobinson\", waving Union Jack flags and holding signs that read \"I\n[love] my country\". Officers have moved people off the beach because\nsome anti-immigration protesters pushed through the police line and ran up the\nseafront. Protesters\nfrom opposing sides are gradually getting closer to each other."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:163e88d5-ba26-4c13-8e43-17fd56f1f235","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"The latest on another day of clashes","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/d6bc7c9b-2d50-4be4-818b-ecc45d688bec.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T17:03:12.970Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T17:03:12.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"There have been several fast-moving developments today - here's the latest:  Stay with us, we'll continue to bring you all the latest updates. "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:3513779d-fbe0-449a-aabe-95f54bc9d0c5","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Rishi Sunak condemns 'criminal behaviour'","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/aff7e0fb-84b2-4a7a-9d97-d366a944ef39.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T16:50:48.705Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:50:48.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Rishi Sunak says\nthe unrest across the country is \"violent, criminal behaviour that has no\nplace in our society.\" The former\nPM, now leader of the opposition, wrote on X that the violence has\n\"nothing to do with the tragedy in Southport\" and added that the\npolice have \"our full support to deal with these criminals swiftly\". "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:8efd678b-9725-40b9-96bf-9810de9fbe6f","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"147 arrests and more to come, police chief says","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T16:32:58.682Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:32:58.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Since Saturday night, police have made 147 arrests across the country, and that number is expected to rise, BJ Harrington of the National Police Chiefs’ Council has said in a statement. He blamed disinformation for driving the \"appalling violence\", adding that \"often posts are being shared and amplified by high profile accounts\". He also had a warning for those involved in the unrest: “Intelligence teams, detectives and neighbourhood officers are working round the clock to identify and apprehend those involved and make no mistake, if you haven’t had a knock on the door yet, your time will come.\" "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:457dfa8a-36bf-41f3-b3db-6957045ebbe0","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Car set alight and windows smashed as unrest continues in Middlesbrough","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/04f8f151-9ce4-490e-a1ae-6d7bb6fd4bd3.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T16:21:04.287Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:21:03.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"We've seen crowds gather today in Rotherham, Bolton, Hull, Southport, Middlesbrough, and elsewhere, with varying levels of unrest. In Middlesbrough, the BBC team has witnessed demonstrators carry a banner referring to the Southport knife attacks, before throwing cans and bottles at police officers and shops with the shutters down. Youths wearing masks have attacked cars, with one overturned and set alight.  Cars and homes in side streets around the town centre have also been targeted, their windows smashed. Wheelie bins have also been set alight. The local police force, Cleveland Police, say they've made nine arrests so far.  People are being advised to stay away from the area."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:c97e9a52-5905-4741-985a-cb06800204d5","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Faith leaders say 'there is no place for hate'","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T16:13:25.978Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:13:25.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Faith leaders across Merseyside have said \"too many people have sought to use the [Southport] tragedy to create division and hate\". Leaders from the Catholic, Muslim, Jewish, Sikh and Hindu communities, among others, issued a joint statement this afternoon.  \"Division can destroy the very relationships and environment that we depend upon every day of our lives and there is no place for hate in our communities,\" it read. The statement called for \"community spirit\" and urged people to remember that \"there is far more that unites than divides us\"."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:2d293a5a-6d9c-407a-8e82-6a60514701d2","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Mosques will get new 'rapid response' security, Home Office says","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T16:07:21.719Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:07:21.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"The Home Office has just announced a new security scheme for mosques. It says mosques \"at risk of violent disorder\" will now be able to request \"rapid response\" personnel. \"The police, local authorities and mosques can ask for rapid security to be deployed, protecting communities and allowing for a return to worship as quickly as possible,\" a Home Office statement says. The new security measures will \"build on\" the government's existing Protective Security for Mosques Scheme, which already funds security at mosques and Muslim schools. Home Secretary Yvette Cooper said: “As a nation we will not tolerate criminal behaviour, dangerous extremism, and racist attacks that go against everything our country stands for.” Earlier, PM Keir Starmer called unrest in UK streets \"far-right thuggery\", and said that Muslim communities had been targeted."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:921c5731-dc98-4442-97c8-382516495e09","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"'I know how frightening this must be'","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/3737fba3-96da-49ef-8ecc-89570653b943.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T15:54:54.922Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T15:54:54.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Starmer described the scenes in Rotherham as \"marauding gangs intent on law-breaking and nothing else\" and said they left \"residents and staff in absolute fear\". To those that feel targeted, he said: \"I know how frightening it must be.\"  He promised that the \"violent mob do not represent our country\" and said: \"If you target people because of the colour of their skin or faith, that is far-right and I’m prepared to say so.\" \"Our police deserve our support as they tackle any and all violent disorder that flares up,\" he added.   The PM said he had held meetings throughout the weekend to make sure the \"necessary support\" is in place."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:5368665b-45aa-45c8-8ba9-ebefe26f9ce1","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Starmer promises convictions to 'bring thugs to justice'","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T15:46:21.970Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T15:46:33.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Starmer vowed to do \"whatever it takes to bring these thugs to justice\" as he addressed the nation following ongoing unrest across parts of the country. He said there will be arrests, there will be charges and there will be convictions, \"whatever the apparent cause or motivation\".  \"This is not a protest, it is organised, violent thuggery and it has no place on our streets or online,\" he said.  He specifically condemned an attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham this afternoon.  The PM said those participating in violence, including those \"whipping up this action online\", would regret it."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:1e537bd2-42e2-46bb-9dc2-db5e92a76414","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"PM condemns 'far-right thuggery'","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T15:39:07.514Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:19:28.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Keir Starmer has condemned what he has called \"far-right thuggery\" in the form of violent demonstrations.  Speaking to reporters at Downing Street just now, the prime minister said those involved will feel the \"full force of the law\".  \"You will regret taking part in this,\" he told those involved in the unrest. \"There is no justification for taking this action.\" He said people in this country \"have a right to be safe\" and yet we've seen \"Muslim communities targeted... Nazi salutes in the street\".  \"I won't shy away from calling it what it is - far-right thuggery,\" Starmer said.  "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:8636ca35-f4d0-45c4-b8b6-1551deb4341c","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Rotherham violence 'utterly appalling' says Cooper","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T15:31:15.046Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T15:31:14.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Home Secretary Yvette Cooper has posted on X about the scenes in Rotherham. \"The criminal, violent attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham is utterly appalling,\" she says.  \"Deliberately setting fire to a building with people known to be inside. \n\nSouth Yorkshire Police have full Government support for the strongest action against those responsible.\""}]},{"@context":"http://schema.org","@type":"WebPage","description":"The PM says people \"have a right to be safe\" and yet we've seen \"Muslim communities targeted... edrdn950"/></span></span><span><strong>Jamie Whitehead</strong><br/>Live reporter</span></p>
      <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">We're bringing our coverage of the violence seen in several towns around England to a close now. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">A riot in Rotherham was followed by violent scenes in Middlesbrough, with chanting between rival groups and one arrest. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">If you want more on this story: </p><ul class="ssrcss-1ynsflq-UnorderedList e1q8fsc70"><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      Starmer condemns "<a href="https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cpwddpzyxpzo" class="ssrcss-f6h2dj-InlineLink e1kn3p7n0">far right thuggery"</a> after more violence
    </li><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      Southport has <a href="https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cg3mmnw4m3zo" class="ssrcss-f6h2dj-InlineLink e1kn3p7n0">'mixed emotions</a>' as disorder follows attack 
    </li><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      Opposing protest groups separated by police in <a href="https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cw8yy9e2j3qo" class="ssrcss-f6h2dj-InlineLink e1kn3p7n0">Weymouth </a>
    </li><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      <a href="https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn088186zgno" class="ssrcss-f6h2dj-InlineLink e1kn3p7n0">And in Hull</a>, the community shows trouble "does not represent" the city 
    </li></ul><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Thank you for joining us today, the coverage was bought to you by Sophie Abdullah, Seher Asaf, Ruth Comerford, Sean Seddon, Zahra Fatima, Lucy Clarke-Billings, Tiffany Wertheimer, George Bowden, Sam Horti, Tom McArthur and me. </p>

type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>A roundup of what's happened today</span><span August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header>
      <figure class="ssrcss-12gl1u2-StyledFigure e34k3c22"><div class="ssrcss-ab5fd8-StyledFigureContainer e34k3c21"><span class="ssrcss-17diaxr-Placeholder etlorgc0"><img alt="A man throwing a brick with a fire behind him" src="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2a041f20-9059-498d-9ae3-8ec28adf5ffd.jpg.webp" srcSet="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/240/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2a041f20-9059-498d-9ae3-8ec28adf5ffd.jpg.webp 240w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/320/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2a041f20-9059-498d-9ae3-8ec28adf5ffd.jpg.webp 320w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/480/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2a041f20-9059-498d-9ae3-8ec28adf5ffd.jpg.webp 480w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/624/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2a041f20-9059-498d-9ae3-8ec28adf5ffd.jpg.webp 624w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/800/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2a041f20-9059-498d-9ae3-8ec28adf5ffd.jpg.webp 800w" width="1000" height="666" class="ssrcss-11yxrdo-Image edrdn950"/></span><span role="text" class="ssrcss-2z7a81-StyledFigureCopyright e34k3c20"><span class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">Image source, </span>Reuters</span></div></figure><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">There’s been a lot going
around England today, let’s bring you up to speed on some of Sunday’s top
lines:</p><ul class="ssrcss-1ynsflq-UnorderedList e1q8fsc70"><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      At least
     ten police officers were injured during a demonstration which turned into
     a riot at the Holiday Inn Express hotel in Rotherham. A
     large bin was set alight, and we also saw protestors smashing windows and
     doors, before managing to get inside the building where asylum seekers are
     staying
    </li></ul><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p><ul class="ssrcss-1ynsflq-UnorderedList e1q8fsc70"><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      At
     least one car was overturned and set on fire in Middlesbrough as
     demonstraters managed to break free of the police. Burning wheelie bins
     were pushed towards officers as the group marched through the town. Around
     300 people gathered to protect a mosque in the town
    </li></ul><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p><ul class="ssrcss-1ynsflq-UnorderedList e1q8fsc70"><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      Police contained around 400 protestors from opposing sides who were
     gathered on Weymouth seafront. One person has
     been arrested
    </li></ul><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p><ul class="ssrcss-1ynsflq-UnorderedList e1q8fsc70"><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned "far-right thuggery" on UK streets and has promised swift
     convictions for those responsible. He also announced emergency security
     will be offered to mosques at risk of violence
    </li></ul><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p><ul class="ssrcss-1ynsflq-UnorderedList e1q8fsc70"><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      And two men have been charged with committing violent disorder in Liverpool yesterday, Merseyside Police say 
    </li></ul><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p>
    </article></div><div class="ssrcss-18z0lpw-PostActions e1ua5vbq1"><div></div><div data-testid="share-tools-container"><div data-testid="information-panel" id="asset:71c7839d-117c-473a-87c6-f98e27122617" class="ssrcss-1wmuay-ContentPost e6wdqbx1"><header class="ssrcss-1p40248-Header e14e9ror5"><span class="ssrcss-1de7kuz-HeadingContainer e14e9ror1"><h3 type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>Swinney warns against 'unhelpful speculation' after Scotland stabbing</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">published at 20:13 BST 4 August 2024</span><div class="ssrcss-8ksoz0-Wrapper ekpio9q3"><span aria-hidden="true" class="ssrcss-crgz56-Time ekpio9q2">20:13 BST 4 August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header>
      <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Turning to Scotland, where the First Minister John Swinney has warned against the "unhelpful speculation" surrounding the identity of a man who allegedly stabbed a 21-year-old woman in the central Scottish city of Stirling on Saturday.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">She was treated in hospital for "non-life threatening" injuries, and a 29-year old man has been arrested.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">However, social media rumours, promoted by EDL founder Tommy Robinson, suggested the attacker was Muslim.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Police Scotland have said these claims are false, and in quite an unusual move for the force, have confirmed the man arrested is white and from the local area.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Swinney says the incident is "isolated", but his predecessor, Humza Yousaf is now urging PM Keir Starmer to call in the army to try and quell some of the violence we're seeing in places like Rotherham.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">He wrote on X: "How much worse does it have to get before the army is sent in Keir Starmer? The police clearly do not have a handle on this situation".</p>
    </article></div><div class="ssrcss-18z0lpw-PostActions e1ua5vbq1"><div></div><div data-testid="share-tools-container"><div data-testid="information-panel" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>Today is nothing different for Weymouth and Portland</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">published at 19:50 BST 4 August 2024</span><div class="ssrcss-8ksoz0-Wrapper ekpio9q3"><span aria-hidden="true" class="ssrcss-crgz56-Time ekpio9q2">19:50 BST 4 August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header><p class="ssrcss-8kvzjk-Contributor etflsgg2"><span><strong>Sophie Cridland</strong><br/>Reporting from Weymouth</span></p>
      <figure class="ssrcss-12gl1u2-StyledFigure e34k3c22"><div class="ssrcss-ab5fd8-StyledFigureContainer e34k3c21"><span class="ssrcss-9kapsl-Placeholder etlorgc0"><img alt="Police officers line up in front of anti-racism demonstrators in Weymouth" src="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/554e4e87-3c28-472d-bf4a-60196f2ebcf0.jpg.webp" srcSet="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/240/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/554e4e87-3c28-472d-bf4a-60196f2ebcf0.jpg.webp 240w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/320/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/554e4e87-3c28-472d-bf4a-60196f2ebcf0.jpg.webp 320w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/480/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/554e4e87-3c28-472d-bf4a-60196f2ebcf0.jpg.webp 480w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/624/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/554e4e87-3c28-472d-bf4a-60196f2ebcf0.jpg.webp 624w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/800/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/554e4e87-3c28-472d-bf4a-60196f2ebcf0.jpg.webp 800w" width="1000" height="750" class="ssrcss-11yxrdo-Image edrdn950"/></span></div></figure><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">There’s been tensions over
immigration here in Weymouth since April last year, when it was announced that
the Bibby Stockholm would be housing asylum seekers waiting for their
applications to be processed.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">That caused a divide in the community and two groups
formed - but both opposed the barge that
is now berthed at Portland Port.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">One saying they didn’t feel safe with around 500
foreign men aged 18 - 65 being brought in to the community.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">The other group felt it was inhumane to house people on
a barge that they say is similar to a prison.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">It’s been protest after protest here since summer last
year with both groups clashing, so today is nothing different for the community
of Weymouth and Portland.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p>
    </article></div><div class="ssrcss-18z0lpw-PostActions e1ua5vbq1"><div></div><div data-testid="share-tools-container"><div data-testid="information-panel" August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header>
      <figure class="ssrcss-12gl1u2-StyledFigure e34k3c22"><div class="ssrcss-ab5fd8-StyledFigureContainer e34k3c21"><span class="ssrcss-1y71a8i-Placeholder etlorgc0"><img alt="Crowd gathers to guard a mosque" src="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/6a5100f2-9d13-43d3-8e03-66285fbc7f40.jpg.webp" srcSet="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/240/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/6a5100f2-9d13-43d3-8e03-66285fbc7f40.jpg.webp 240w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/320/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/6a5100f2-9d13-43d3-8e03-66285fbc7f40.jpg.webp 320w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/480/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/6a5100f2-9d13-43d3-8e03-66285fbc7f40.jpg.webp 480w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/624/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/6a5100f2-9d13-43d3-8e03-66285fbc7f40.jpg.webp 624w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/800/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/6a5100f2-9d13-43d3-8e03-66285fbc7f40.jpg.webp 800w" width="1024" height="683" class="ssrcss-11yxrdo-Image edrdn950"/></span><span role="text" class="ssrcss-2z7a81-StyledFigureCopyright e34k3c20"><span class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">Image source, </span>Ian Forsyth/ Stringer</span></div></figure><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Earlier today around 300 people gathered to guard the
Jamia Masjid al-Madinah mosque in Middlesbrough, as anti-immigration demonstrations
marched through the town.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">At
least one car has been set alight in the Teesside town and burning wheelie bins
have been pushed toward police officers.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"We're going to be paying for this, and for
what?" one resident told PA News Agency.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"This has nothing to do with three little
girls that have lost their lives. They've used it to cause utter mayhem on our
streets".</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Cleveland Police say 14 arrests have been
made so far. </p>
    </article></div><div class="ssrcss-18z0lpw-PostActions e1ua5vbq1"><div></div><div data-testid="share-tools-container"><div data-testid="information-panel" type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>Protesters contained in Weymouth</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header><p class="ssrcss-8kvzjk-Contributor etflsgg2"><span><strong>Sophie Cridland</strong><br/>Reporting from Weymouth</span></p>
      <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Police have
contained protesters here on Weymouth esplanade.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Around 30
police from Dorset and the neighbouring counties of Devon and Cornwall are
managing the groups.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Both sides
are still chanting with one shouting “save our streets” and the other “shame on
you”</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Traffic is
now moving and the buses and cars that were at a standstill have moved on with
the road fully open around the King’s Statue.
Police riot vans are still surrounding the pedestrianised sea front blocking in
the protestors, away from members of the public.</p>
    </article></div><div class="ssrcss-18z0lpw-PostActions e1ua5vbq1"><div></div><div data-testid="share-tools-container"><div data-testid="information-panel" type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>Ten police officers injured in Rotherham hotel violence</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">published at 19:12 BST 4 August 2024</span><div class="ssrcss-8ksoz0-Wrapper ekpio9q3"><span aria-hidden="true" class="ssrcss-crgz56-Time ekpio9q2">19:12 BST 4 August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header>
      <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">South Yorkshire Police (SYP) says at least 10 officers were injured in the "deplorable acts of violence" at the Holiday Inn Express Hotel in Rotherham earlier today.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">One officer was left unconscious after a head injury, SYP says, with other officers suffering suspected broken bones, including a suspected fractured elbow.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">There have so far been no reports of any hotel staff or residents being injured in the disorder.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"We absolutely condemn the scenes of disorder we witnessed, with a total of around 700 people in attendance in Manvers Way earlier today," a statement says. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"A large bin close to a window of the hotel was also set alight causing a small fire which was later extinguished, with missiles, including glass bottles and beer cans, thrown at our officers."</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p><figure class="ssrcss-12gl1u2-StyledFigure e34k3c22"><div class="ssrcss-ab5fd8-StyledFigureContainer e34k3c21"><span class="ssrcss-bswy09-Placeholder etlorgc0"><img alt="Two masked men - one draped in an England flag - challenge police during violence outside a hotel in Rotherham." src="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/f5dcbe90-1e17-4ba0-8dec-94305953586f.jpg.webp" srcSet="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/240/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/f5dcbe90-1e17-4ba0-8dec-94305953586f.jpg.webp 240w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/320/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/f5dcbe90-1e17-4ba0-8dec-94305953586f.jpg.webp 320w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/480/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/f5dcbe90-1e17-4ba0-8dec-94305953586f.jpg.webp 480w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/624/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/f5dcbe90-1e17-4ba0-8dec-94305953586f.jpg.webp 624w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/800/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/f5dcbe90-1e17-4ba0-8dec-94305953586f.jpg.webp 800w" width="2899" height="1631" class="ssrcss-11yxrdo-Image edrdn950"/></span><span role="text" class="ssrcss-2z7a81-StyledFigureCopyright e34k3c20"><span class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">Image source, </span>PA</span></div><figcaption class="ssrcss-zg069t-StyledFigureCaption e1xtk7to0"><span class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">Image caption, </span><div spacing="6" class="ssrcss-1ml6m0y-Stack e1y4nx260"><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">A crowd chanting "get them out" smashed windows at the Holiday Inn Express hotel.</p></div></figcaption></figure>

e2hr5uu0"/><h3 type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>Will Starmer's tough talk be enough to quell the unrest?</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">published at 18:47 BST 4 August 2024</span><div class="ssrcss-8ksoz0-Wrapper ekpio9q3"><span aria-hidden="true" class="ssrcss-crgz56-Time ekpio9q2">18:47 BST 4 August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header><p class="ssrcss-8kvzjk-Contributor etflsgg2"><span class="ssrcss-1gp5o89-ImageWrapper etflsgg1"><span class="ssrcss-1105681-Placeholder etlorgc0"><img alt="" loading="lazy" src="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/128/cpsprodpb//vivo/live/images/2024/3/7/8a0a1cd6-8a14-45af-b1e5-566c143fab9e.jpg.webp" width="64" height="64" class="ssrcss-11yxrdo-Image edrdn950"/></span></span><span><strong>Hannah Miller</strong><br/>Political correspondent</span></p>
      <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Keir Starmer has sounded tough on the violent
disorder for days - today branding it "far-right thuggery".</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">His warning to those taking part that they will
regret it extended not just to those committing criminal acts on the streets,
but also those stirring it up online.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Mosques have been offered emergency security, extra
prosecutors have been brought in, and ministerial meetings have been held over
the weekend.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">But still the violence continues, and it remains to
be seen whether tough language will bring it to an end.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">The government believes that once people see
arrests, charges and convictions becoming a reality, they will think twice
about taking part.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">For
communities and individuals who feel at risk, it has already been going on too
long.</p>
    </article></div><div class="ssrcss-18z0lpw-PostActions e1ua5vbq1"><div></div><div data-testid="share-tools-container"><div data-testid="information-panel" type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>More police arrive in Weymouth</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header><p class="ssrcss-8kvzjk-Contributor etflsgg2"><span><strong>Sophie Cridland</strong><br/>Reporting from Weymouth</span></p>
      <figure class="ssrcss-12gl1u2-StyledFigure e34k3c22"><div class="ssrcss-ab5fd8-StyledFigureContainer e34k3c21"><span class="ssrcss-9kapsl-Placeholder etlorgc0"><img alt="A line of riot police in Weymouth" src="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2791ca4a-db67-40bc-86d6-908aad6261b0.jpg.webp" srcSet="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/240/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2791ca4a-db67-40bc-86d6-908aad6261b0.jpg.webp 240w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/320/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2791ca4a-db67-40bc-86d6-908aad6261b0.jpg.webp 320w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/480/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2791ca4a-db67-40bc-86d6-908aad6261b0.jpg.webp 480w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/624/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2791ca4a-db67-40bc-86d6-908aad6261b0.jpg.webp 624w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/800/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2791ca4a-db67-40bc-86d6-908aad6261b0.jpg.webp 800w" width="2776" height="2082" class="ssrcss-11yxrdo-Image edrdn950"/></span></div></figure><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Dorset Police have built a physical wall using
themselves and about five riot vans to stop the two protest groups meeting on
the road and the esplanade.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">The anti-immigration group is slowly moving towards
Stand up to Racism Dorset, which the police are trying to stop. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">They’ve called in more resources and in the last 10
minutes, more police vans have turned up.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Traffic
is at a standstill in the town centre with buses and cars trying to leave the
area, but they are stuck waiting at the King’s Statue.</p>

type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>Protesters face off in Weymouth</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">published at 18:13 BST 4 August 2024</span><div class="ssrcss-8ksoz0-Wrapper ekpio9q3"><span aria-hidden="true" class="ssrcss-crgz56-Time ekpio9q2">18:13 BST 4 August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header><p class="ssrcss-8kvzjk-Contributor etflsgg2"><span><strong>Sophie Cridland</strong><br/>Reporting from Weymouth</span></p>
      <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">About 400 protestors from opposing sides are
gathered on Weymouth seafront chanting at each other.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">On one side, the crowd is chanting "Weymouth
and Portland welcome refugees" with placards saying "Nazis go
home".</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">The other group are chanting "Tommy
Robinson", waving Union Jack flags and holding signs that read "I
[love] my country".</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Officers have moved people off the beach because
some anti-immigration protesters pushed through the police line and ran up the
seafront.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Protesters
from opposing sides are gradually getting closer to each other.</p><figure class="ssrcss-12gl1u2-StyledFigure e34k3c22"><div class="ssrcss-ab5fd8-StyledFigureContainer e34k3c21"><span class="ssrcss-w01rjw-Placeholder etlorgc0"><img alt="Police in a line on the beach, walking along" src="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/fc470400-f100-44df-840c-a190d6b5a6eb.png.webp" srcSet="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/240/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/fc470400-f100-44df-840c-a190d6b5a6eb.png.webp 240w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/320/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/fc470400-f100-44df-840c-a190d6b5a6eb.png.webp 320w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/480/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/fc470400-f100-44df-840c-a190d6b5a6eb.png.webp 480w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/624/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/fc470400-f100-44df-840c-a190d6b5a6eb.png.webp 624w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/800/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/fc470400-f100-44df-840c-a190d6b5a6eb.png.webp 800w" width="1163" height="642" class="ssrcss-11yxrdo-Image edrdn950"/></span></div><figcaption class="ssrcss-zg069t-StyledFigureCaption e1xtk7to0"><span class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">Image caption, </span><div spacing="6" class="ssrcss-1ml6m0y-Stack e1y4nx260"><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Police have asked people to leave Weymouth Beach</p></div></figcaption></figure>

type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>The latest on another day of clashes</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">published at 18:03 BST 4 August 2024</span><div class="ssrcss-8ksoz0-Wrapper ekpio9q3"><span aria-hidden="true" class="ssrcss-crgz56-Time ekpio9q2">18:03 BST 4 August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header>
      <figure class="ssrcss-12gl1u2-StyledFigure e34k3c22"><div class="ssrcss-ab5fd8-StyledFigureContainer e34k3c21"><span class="ssrcss-sh18lg-Placeholder etlorgc0"><img alt="A crowd of people watch on as a man fights with three police officers" src="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/d6bc7c9b-2d50-4be4-818b-ecc45d688bec.jpg.webp" srcSet="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/240/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/d6bc7c9b-2d50-4be4-818b-ecc45d688bec.jpg.webp 240w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/320/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/d6bc7c9b-2d50-4be4-818b-ecc45d688bec.jpg.webp 320w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/480/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/d6bc7c9b-2d50-4be4-818b-ecc45d688bec.jpg.webp 480w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/624/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/d6bc7c9b-2d50-4be4-818b-ecc45d688bec.jpg.webp 624w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/800/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/d6bc7c9b-2d50-4be4-818b-ecc45d688bec.jpg.webp 800w" width="1000" height="563" class="ssrcss-11yxrdo-Image edrdn950"/></span><span role="text" class="ssrcss-2z7a81-StyledFigureCopyright e34k3c20"><span class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">Image source, </span>PA Media</span></div></figure><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">There have been several fast-moving developments today - here's the latest:</p><ul class="ssrcss-1ynsflq-UnorderedList e1q8fsc70"><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      In <b class="ssrcss-cgjb6m-Keyword e2vephq0">Rotherham</b>, hundreds of anti-immigration protesters surrounded the Holiday Inn Express hotel, where some people lit a fire, smashed windows and doors and managed to get inside the building where asylum seekers were staying. Riot police surrounded the hotel and pushed crowds back, but the scene there remains tense
    </li><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      Crowds in <b class="ssrcss-cgjb6m-Keyword e2vephq0">Middlesbrough </b>set fire to an overturned car and pushed burning wheelie bins at police
    </li><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      A protest in <b class="ssrcss-cgjb6m-Keyword e2vephq0">Bolton</b> started off peacefully, but then became heated when two groups clashed. One, most with faces covered by face masks or balaclavas - charged towards town hall shouting "Allahu Akbar" - or God is greatest. They clashed with a separate group waving England flags
    </li><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      There were heated scenes in <b class="ssrcss-cgjb6m-Keyword e2vephq0">Bolton </b>where two groups of protesters clashed
    </li><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      There have also been protests in <b class="ssrcss-cgjb6m-Keyword e2vephq0">Hull</b> and <b class="ssrcss-cgjb6m-Keyword e2vephq0">Weymouth</b>
    </li></ul><ul class="ssrcss-1ynsflq-UnorderedList e1q8fsc70"><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      PM Keir Starmer has condemned the <b class="ssrcss-cgjb6m-Keyword e2vephq0">"far-right
     thuggery" </b>and vowed to do "whatever it takes to bring
     these thugs to justice"
    </li></ul><ul class="ssrcss-1ynsflq-UnorderedList e1q8fsc70"><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      The Home Office says that <b class="ssrcss-cgjb6m-Keyword e2vephq0">mosques will be offered greater
     protection</b> with new "emergency security" measures that
     can be rapidly deployed where needed
    </li><li spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260">
      And <b class="ssrcss-cgjb6m-Keyword e2vephq0">147 arrests </b>have been made across the country since
     Saturday night, and police are expecting that number to rise
    </li></ul><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Stay with us, we'll continue to bring you all the latest updates. </p>
    </article></div><div class="ssrcss-18z0lpw-PostActions e1ua5vbq1"><div></div><div data-testid="share-tools-container"><div data-testid="information-panel" the unrest across the country is "violent, criminal behaviour that has no
place in our society."</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">The former
PM, now leader of the opposition, <a href="https://x.com/RishiSunak/status/1820132969513664691" class="ssrcss-f6h2dj-InlineLink e1kn3p7n0">wrote on X<span class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">, <!-- -->external</span></a> that the violence has

police have "our full support to deal with these criminals swiftly".</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p>

type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>147 arrests and more to come, police chief says</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden August 2024</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header>
      <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Since Saturday night, police have made 147 arrests across the country, and that number is expected to rise, BJ Harrington of the National Police Chiefs’ Council has said <a href="https://news.npcc.police.uk/releases/public-order-lead-confirms-arrests-and-denounces-disinformation" class="ssrcss-f6h2dj-InlineLink e1kn3p7n0">in a statement<span class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">, <!-- -->external</span></a>.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">He blamed disinformation for driving the "appalling violence", adding that "often posts are being shared and amplified by high profile accounts".</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">He also had a warning for those involved in the unrest:</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">“Intelligence teams, detectives and neighbourhood officers are working round the clock to identify and apprehend those involved and make no mistake, if you haven’t had a knock on the door yet, your time will come."</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p>

type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>Car set alight and windows smashed as unrest continues in Middlesbrough</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">published at 17:21 BST 4 August 2024</span><div class="ssrcss-8ksoz0-Wrapper ekpio9q3"><span aria-hidden="true"       <figure class="ssrcss-12gl1u2-StyledFigure e34k3c22"><div class="ssrcss-ab5fd8-StyledFigureContainer e34k3c21"><span class="ssrcss-b8s61n-Placeholder etlorgc0"><img alt="A car burns after being overturned during an anti-immigration protest in Middlesbrough. Picture date: Sunday August 4, 2024" src="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/04f8f151-9ce4-490e-a1ae-6d7bb6fd4bd3.jpg.webp" srcSet="https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/240/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/04f8f151-9ce4-490e-a1ae-6d7bb6fd4bd3.jpg.webp 240w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/320/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/04f8f151-9ce4-490e-a1ae-6d7bb6fd4bd3.jpg.webp 320w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/480/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/04f8f151-9ce4-490e-a1ae-6d7bb6fd4bd3.jpg.webp 480w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/624/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/04f8f151-9ce4-490e-a1ae-6d7bb6fd4bd3.jpg.webp 624w, https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/800/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/04f8f151-9ce4-490e-a1ae-6d7bb6fd4bd3.jpg.webp 800w" width="1000" height="667" class="ssrcss-11yxrdo-Image edrdn950"/></span><span role="text" class="ssrcss-2z7a81-StyledFigureCopyright e34k3c20"><span class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">Image source, </span>PA Media</span></div></figure><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">We've seen crowds gather today in Rotherham, Bolton, Hull, Southport, Middlesbrough, and elsewhere, with varying levels of unrest.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">In Middlesbrough, the BBC team has witnessed demonstrators carry a banner referring to the Southport knife attacks, before throwing cans and bottles at police officers and shops with the shutters down.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Youths wearing masks have attacked cars, with one overturned and set alight. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Cars and homes in side streets around the town centre have also been targeted, their windows smashed. Wheelie bins have also been set alight.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">The local police force, Cleveland Police, say they've made nine arrests so far. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">People are being advised to stay away from the area.</p><figure class="ssrcss-12gl1u2-StyledFigure e34k3c22"><div class="ssrcss-ab5fd8-StyledFigureContainer e34k3c21"><span class="ssrcss-b8s61n-Placeholder etlorgc0"><img alt="Police officers during an anti-immigration protest in Middlesbrough.
