class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>Faith leaders say 'there is no place for hate'</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">published at 17:13 BST 4 August 2024</span><div       <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Faith leaders across Merseyside have said "too many people have sought to use the [Southport] tragedy to create division and hate".</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Leaders from the Catholic, Muslim, Jewish, Sikh and Hindu communities, among others, issued a joint statement this afternoon. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"Division can destroy the very relationships and environment that we depend upon every day of our lives and there is no place for hate in our communities," it read.</p><div class="ssrcss-76apf6-QuoteWrapper e1huhdtf0"><blockquote class="ssrcss-givbdw-QuoteBlock e13av51i1"><div spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-131sxq9-Stack e1y4nx260"><span class="visually-hidden ssrcss-1f39n02-VisuallyHidden e16en2lz0">Quote Message</span><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">It can - and has - left communities in fear and has put people in danger.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"><b class="ssrcss-cgjb6m-Keyword e2vephq0"></b></p></div></blockquote></div><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">The statement called for "community spirit" and urged people to remember that "there is far more that unites than divides us".</p>

type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>Mosques will get new 'rapid response' security, Home Office says</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-1yk290u-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden August 2024</span><span class="ssrcss-8xw9k4-Breaking ekpio9q1">Breaking</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header>
      <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">The Home Office has just announced a new security scheme for mosques. It says mosques "at risk of violent disorder" will now be able to request "rapid response" personnel.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"The police, local authorities and mosques can ask for rapid security to be deployed, protecting communities and allowing for a return to worship as quickly as possible," a Home Office statement says.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">The new security measures will "build on" the government's existing Protective Security for Mosques Scheme, which already funds security at mosques and Muslim schools.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Home Secretary Yvette Cooper said: “As a nation we will not tolerate criminal behaviour, dangerous extremism, and racist attacks that go against everything our country stands for.”</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Earlier, PM Keir Starmer called unrest in UK streets "far-right thuggery", and said that Muslim communities had been targeted.</p>

class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">He promised that the "violent mob do not represent our country" and said: "If you target people because of the colour of their skin or faith, that is far-right and I’m prepared to say so."</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"Our police deserve our support as they tackle any and all violent disorder that flares up," he added. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p><div class="ssrcss-76apf6-QuoteWrapper e1huhdtf0"><blockquote class="ssrcss-givbdw-QuoteBlock e13av51i1"><div spacing="rich-text" class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-umer6k-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden       <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Starmer vowed to do "whatever it takes to bring these thugs to justice" as he addressed the nation following ongoing unrest across parts of the country.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">He said there will be arrests, there will be charges and there will be convictions, "whatever the apparent cause or motivation". </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"This is not a protest, it is organised, violent thuggery and it has no place on our streets or online," he said. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">He specifically condemned an attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham this afternoon. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">The PM said those participating in violence, including those "whipping up this action online", would regret it.</p>

played</p></div></div></noscript></div></div></figure></div><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Keir Starmer has condemned what he has called "far-right thuggery" in the form of violent demonstrations. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Speaking to reporters at Downing Street just now, the prime minister said those involved will feel the "full force of the law". </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"You will regret taking part in this," he told those involved in the unrest.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"There is no justification for taking this action."</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">He said people in this country "have a right to be safe" and yet we've seen "Muslim communities targeted... Nazi salutes in the street". </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"I won't shy away from calling it what it is - far-right thuggery," Starmer said. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10"></p>

type="normal" class="ssrcss-ad2rmd-Heading e10rt3ze0"><span role="text" class="ssrcss-189b1h2-HeadlineWrap e14e9ror2"><span>Rotherham violence 'utterly appalling' says Cooper</span><span class="ssrcss-etauer-ServerSideTime e14e9ror0"><span data-testid="timestamp" class="ssrcss-1yk290u-Timestamp ekpio9q4"><span data-testid="accessible-timestamp" class="visually-hidden August 2024</span><span class="ssrcss-8xw9k4-Breaking ekpio9q1">Breaking</span></div></span></span></span></h3></span></header>
      <p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">Home Secretary Yvette Cooper has posted on X about the scenes in Rotherham.</p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"The criminal, violent attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham is utterly appalling," she says. </p><p class="ssrcss-1q0x1qg-Paragraph e1jhz7w10">"Deliberately setting fire to a building with people known to be inside. 

South Yorkshire Police have full Government support for the strongest action against those responsible."</p>

# Starmer condemns 'far-right thuggery' on UK streets and says those involved 'will regret it'

3 August 2024![three riot police](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/1024/cpsprodpb/58b3/live/1bb27e90-5262-11ef-8f0f-0577398c3339.jpg)

## Summary

- PM Keir Starmer condemns "far-right thuggery" on UK streets and says those involved "will regret it"
- [His comments](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset%3A457dfa8a-36bf-41f3-b3db-6957045ebbe0#post) follow violence in several cities on Saturday and further unrest on Sunday
- Earlier, a crowd chanting "get them out" smashed windows at the Holiday Inn Express hotel in [Rotherham and started a fire](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset%3A8267deb9-643b-42bb-acec-351c5ec2e408#post)
- Police officers with riot shields surrounded the building and tried to push people back - and extinguished the fire
- More than 140 people have been arrested in relation to disorder since Saturday night
- There are also demonstrations in Bolton, Weymouth, Southport and [Middlesbrough](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset%3A457dfa8a-36bf-41f3-b3db-6957045ebbe0#post), where the crowd broke through a police line
- Protests organised by the far-right started across the country after the fatal stabbing of three young girls in Southport on Monday

## Live Reporting

Edited by Jamie Whitehead and Tom McArthur

It’s been protest after protest here since summer last\nyear with both groups clashing, so today is nothing different for the community\nof Weymouth and Portland. "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:3efc9dcf-799f-4690-8e0d-e67ab6de2e37","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Crowds gather to protect Middlesbrough mosque","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/6a5100f2-9d13-43d3-8e03-66285fbc7f40.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T18:34:47.433Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T19:33:56.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Earlier today around 300 people gathered to guard the\nJamia Masjid al-Madinah mosque in Middlesbrough, as anti-immigration demonstrations\nmarched through the town. At\nleast one car has been set alight in the Teesside town and burning wheelie bins\nhave been pushed toward police officers. \"We're going to be paying for this, and for\nwhat?\" one resident told PA News Agency. \"This has nothing to do with three little\ngirls that have lost their lives. They've used it to cause utter mayhem on our\nstreets\". Cleveland Police say 14 arrests have been\nmade so far. "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:d0aae887-55c8-44c1-8a6b-978530fcaea9","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Protesters contained in Weymouth","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T18:23:50.363Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T18:23:50.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Police have\ncontained protesters here on Weymouth esplanade. Around 30\npolice from Dorset and the neighbouring counties of Devon and Cornwall are\nmanaging the groups. Both sides\nare still chanting with one shouting “save our streets” and the other “shame on\nyou” Traffic is\nnow moving and the buses and cars that were at a standstill have moved on with\nthe road fully open around the King’s Statue.\nPolice riot vans are still surrounding the pedestrianised sea front blocking in\nthe protestors, away from members of the public."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:26e2e5ca-f7b3-4529-9b8e-f06827ed9681","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Ten police officers injured in Rotherham hotel violence","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/f5dcbe90-1e17-4ba0-8dec-94305953586f.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T18:12:01.171Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T18:14:53.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"South Yorkshire Police (SYP) says at least 10 officers were injured in the \"deplorable acts of violence\" at the Holiday Inn Express Hotel in Rotherham earlier today. One officer was left unconscious after a head injury, SYP says, with other officers suffering suspected broken bones, including a suspected fractured elbow. There have so far been no reports of any hotel staff or residents being injured in the disorder. \"We absolutely condemn the scenes of disorder we witnessed, with a total of around 700 people in attendance in Manvers Way earlier today,\" a statement says. \"A large bin close to a window of the hotel was also set alight causing a small fire which was later extinguished, with missiles, including glass bottles and beer cans, thrown at our officers.\" "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:f5be4b0f-1658-43b3-bf31-15dbdcf94a05","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Will Starmer's tough talk be enough to quell the unrest?","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T17:47:12.285Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T17:47:11.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Keir Starmer has sounded tough on the violent\ndisorder for days - today branding it \"far-right thuggery\". His warning to those taking part that they will\nregret it extended not just to those committing criminal acts on the streets,\nbut also those stirring it up online. Mosques have been offered emergency security, extra\nprosecutors have been brought in, and ministerial meetings have been held over\nthe weekend. But still the violence continues, and it remains to\nbe seen whether tough language will bring it to an end. The government believes that once people see\narrests, charges and convictions becoming a reality, they will think twice\nabout taking part. For\ncommunities and individuals who feel at risk, it has already been going on too\nlong."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:d0c87736-3ff3-4072-9de5-779b7c8dd7f0","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"More police arrive in Weymouth","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2791ca4a-db67-40bc-86d6-908aad6261b0.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T17:27:18.223Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T17:35:18.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Dorset Police have built a physical wall using\nthemselves and about five riot vans to stop the two protest groups meeting on\nthe road and the esplanade. The anti-immigration group is slowly moving towards\nStand up to Racism Dorset, which the police are trying to stop. They’ve called in more resources and in the last 10\nminutes, more police vans have turned up. Traffic\nis at a standstill in the town centre with buses and cars trying to leave the\narea, but they are stuck waiting at the King’s Statue."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:f9607b97-2f2e-4cec-9fc1-607461858b87","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Protesters face off in Weymouth","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/fc470400-f100-44df-840c-a190d6b5a6eb.png.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T17:13:43.866Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T17:13:43.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"About 400 protestors from opposing sides are\ngathered on Weymouth seafront chanting at each other. On one side, the crowd is chanting \"Weymouth\nand Portland welcome refugees\" with placards saying \"Nazis go\nhome\". The other group are chanting \"Tommy\nRobinson\", waving Union Jack flags and holding signs that read \"I\n[love] my country\". Officers have moved people off the beach because\nsome anti-immigration protesters pushed through the police line and ran up the\nseafront. Protesters\nfrom opposing sides are gradually getting closer to each other."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:163e88d5-ba26-4c13-8e43-17fd56f1f235","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"The latest on another day of clashes","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/d6bc7c9b-2d50-4be4-818b-ecc45d688bec.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T17:03:12.970Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T17:03:12.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"There have been several fast-moving developments today - here's the latest: Stay with us, we'll continue to bring you all the latest updates. "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:3513779d-fbe0-449a-aabe-95f54bc9d0c5","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Rishi Sunak condemns 'criminal behaviour'","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/aff7e0fb-84b2-4a7a-9d97-d366a944ef39.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T16:50:48.705Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:50:48.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Rishi Sunak says\nthe unrest across the country is \"violent, criminal behaviour that has no\nplace in our society.\" The former\nPM, now leader of the opposition, wrote on X that the violence has\n\"nothing to do with the tragedy in Southport\" and added that the\npolice have \"our full support to deal with these criminals swiftly\". "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:8efd678b-9725-40b9-96bf-9810de9fbe6f","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"147 arrests and more to come, police chief says","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T16:32:58.682Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:32:58.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Since Saturday night, police have made 147 arrests across the country, and that number is expected to rise, BJ Harrington of the National Police Chiefs’ Council has said in a statement. He blamed disinformation for driving the \"appalling violence\", adding that \"often posts are being shared and amplified by high profile accounts\". He also had a warning for those involved in the unrest: “Intelligence teams, detectives and neighbourhood officers are working round the clock to identify and apprehend those involved and make no mistake, if you haven’t had a knock on the door yet, your time will come.\" "},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:457dfa8a-36bf-41f3-b3db-6957045ebbe0","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Car set alight and windows smashed as unrest continues in Middlesbrough","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/04f8f151-9ce4-490e-a1ae-6d7bb6fd4bd3.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T16:21:04.287Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:21:03.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"We've seen crowds gather today in Rotherham, Bolton, Hull, Southport, Middlesbrough, and elsewhere, with varying levels of unrest. In Middlesbrough, the BBC team has witnessed demonstrators carry a banner referring to the Southport knife attacks, before throwing cans and bottles at police officers and shops with the shutters down. Youths wearing masks have attacked cars, with one overturned and set alight. Cars and homes in side streets around the town centre have also been targeted, their windows smashed. Wheelie bins have also been set alight. The local police force, Cleveland Police, say they've made nine arrests so far. People are being advised to stay away from the area."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:c97e9a52-5905-4741-985a-cb06800204d5","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Faith leaders say 'there is no place for hate'","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T16:13:25.978Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:13:25.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Faith leaders across Merseyside have said \"too many people have sought to use the [Southport] tragedy to create division and hate\". Leaders from the Catholic, Muslim, Jewish, Sikh and Hindu communities, among others, issued a joint statement this afternoon. \"Division can destroy the very relationships and environment that we depend upon every day of our lives and there is no place for hate in our communities,\" it read. The statement called for \"community spirit\" and urged people to remember that \"there is far more that unites than divides us\"."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:2d293a5a-6d9c-407a-8e82-6a60514701d2","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Mosques will get new 'rapid response' security, Home Office says","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T16:07:21.719Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:07:21.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"The Home Office has just announced a new security scheme for mosques. It says mosques \"at risk of violent disorder\" will now be able to request \"rapid response\" personnel. \"The police, local authorities and mosques can ask for rapid security to be deployed, protecting communities and allowing for a return to worship as quickly as possible,\" a Home Office statement says. The new security measures will \"build on\" the government's existing Protective Security for Mosques Scheme, which already funds security at mosques and Muslim schools. Home Secretary Yvette Cooper said: “As a nation we will not tolerate criminal behaviour, dangerous extremism, and racist attacks that go against everything our country stands for.” Earlier, PM Keir Starmer called unrest in UK streets \"far-right thuggery\", and said that Muslim communities had been targeted."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:921c5731-dc98-4442-97c8-382516495e09","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"'I know how frightening this must be'","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/3737fba3-96da-49ef-8ecc-89570653b943.jpg.webp","datePublished":"2024-08-04T15:54:54.922Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T15:54:54.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Starmer described the scenes in Rotherham as \"marauding gangs intent on law-breaking and nothing else\" and said they left \"residents and staff in absolute fear\". To those that feel targeted, he said: \"I know how frightening it must be.\" He promised that the \"violent mob do not represent our country\" and said: \"If you target people because of the colour of their skin or faith, that is far-right and I’m prepared to say so.\" \"Our police deserve our support as they tackle any and all violent disorder that flares up,\" he added. The PM said he had held meetings throughout the weekend to make sure the \"necessary support\" is in place."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:5368665b-45aa-45c8-8ba9-ebefe26f9ce1","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"Starmer promises convictions to 'bring thugs to justice'","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T15:46:21.970Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T15:46:33.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Starmer vowed to do \"whatever it takes to bring these thugs to justice\" as he addressed the nation following ongoing unrest across parts of the country. He said there will be arrests, there will be charges and there will be convictions, \"whatever the apparent cause or motivation\". \"This is not a protest, it is organised, violent thuggery and it has no place on our streets or online,\" he said. He specifically condemned an attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham this afternoon. The PM said those participating in violence, including those \"whipping up this action online\", would regret it."},{"@type":"BlogPosting","@id":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt?post=asset:1e537bd2-42e2-46bb-9dc2-db5e92a76414","isAccessibleForFree":"true","headline":"PM condemns 'far-right thuggery'","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"author":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"BBC News","url":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png"}},"image":"https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/ws/simorgh-assets/public/news/images/metadata/poster-1024x576.png","datePublished":"2024-08-04T15:39:07.514Z","dateModified":"2024-08-04T16:19:28.000Z","mainEntityOfPage":"https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/c0jqjxe8d1yt","articleBody":"Keir Starmer has condemned what he has called \"far-right thuggery\" in the form of violent demonstrations. Speaking to reporters at Downing Street just now, the prime minister said those involved will feel the \"full force of the law\". \"You will regret taking part in this,\" he told those involved in the unrest. \"There is no justification for taking this action.\" He said people in this country \"have a right to be safe\" and yet we've seen \"Muslim communities targeted... Nazi salutes in the street\". \"I won't shy away from calling it what it is - far-right thuggery,\" Starmer said. 1. ### Another day of chaos across Englandpublished at 20:45 BST 4 August 202420:45 BST 4 August 2024

   ![](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/128/cpsprodpb//vivo/live/images/2023/3/13/c30ea702-5d4e-4d84-afea-041293d17acb.jpg.webp)**Jamie Whitehead**  
   Live reporter

   We're bringing our coverage of the violence seen in several towns around England to a close now.

   A riot in Rotherham was followed by violent scenes in Middlesbrough, with chanting between rival groups and one arrest.

   If you want more on this story:

   - Starmer condemns "[far right thuggery"](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cpwddpzyxpzo) after more violence
   - Southport has ['mixed emotions](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cg3mmnw4m3zo)' as disorder follows attack
   - Opposing protest groups separated by police in [Weymouth](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cw8yy9e2j3qo)
   - [And in Hull](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn088186zgno), the community shows trouble "does not represent" the city

   Thank you for joining us today, the coverage was bought to you by Sophie Abdullah, Seher Asaf, Ruth Comerford, Sean Seddon, Zahra Fatima, Lucy Clarke-Billings, Tiffany Wertheimer, George Bowden, Sam Horti, Tom McArthur and me.

   Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
2. ### A roundup of what's happened todaypublished at 20:39 BST 4 August 202420:39 BST 4 August 2024

   ![A man throwing a brick with a fire behind him](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2a041f20-9059-498d-9ae3-8ec28adf5ffd.jpg.webp)Image source, Reuters

   There’s been a lot going
   around England today, let’s bring you up to speed on some of Sunday’s top
   lines:

   - At least
     ten police officers were injured during a demonstration which turned into
     a riot at the Holiday Inn Express hotel in Rotherham. A
     large bin was set alight, and we also saw protestors smashing windows and
     doors, before managing to get inside the building where asylum seekers are
     staying
   - At
     least one car was overturned and set on fire in Middlesbrough as
     demonstraters managed to break free of the police. Burning wheelie bins
     were pushed towards officers as the group marched through the town. Around
     300 people gathered to protect a mosque in the town
   - Police contained around 400 protestors from opposing sides who were
     gathered on Weymouth seafront. One person has
     been arrested
   - Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned "far-right thuggery" on UK streets and has promised swift
     convictions for those responsible. He also announced emergency security
     will be offered to mosques at risk of violence
   - And two men have been charged with committing violent disorder in Liverpool yesterday, Merseyside Police sayShareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
3. ### Swinney warns against 'unhelpful speculation' after Scotland stabbingpublished at 20:13 BST 4 August 202420:13 BST 4 August 2024

   Turning to Scotland, where the First Minister John Swinney has warned against the "unhelpful speculation" surrounding the identity of a man who allegedly stabbed a 21-year-old woman in the central Scottish city of Stirling on Saturday.

   She was treated in hospital for "non-life threatening" injuries, and a 29-year old man has been arrested.

   However, social media rumours, promoted by EDL founder Tommy Robinson, suggested the attacker was Muslim.

   Police Scotland have said these claims are false, and in quite an unusual move for the force, have confirmed the man arrested is white and from the local area.

   Swinney says the incident is "isolated", but his predecessor, Humza Yousaf is now urging PM Keir Starmer to call in the army to try and quell some of the violence we're seeing in places like Rotherham.

   He wrote on X: "How much worse does it have to get before the army is sent in Keir Starmer? The police clearly do not have a handle on this situation".

   Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
4. ### Today is nothing different for Weymouth and Portlandpublished at 19:50 BST 4 August 202419:50 BST 4 August 2024

   **Sophie Cridland**  
   Reporting from Weymouth

   ![Police officers line up in front of anti-racism demonstrators in Weymouth](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/554e4e87-3c28-472d-bf4a-60196f2ebcf0.jpg.webp)

   There’s been tensions over
   immigration here in Weymouth since April last year, when it was announced that
   the Bibby Stockholm would be housing asylum seekers waiting for their
   applications to be processed.

   That caused a divide in the community and two groups
   formed - but both opposed the barge that
   is now berthed at Portland Port.

   One saying they didn’t feel safe with around 500
   foreign men aged 18 - 65 being brought in to the community.

   The other group felt it was inhumane to house people on
   a barge that they say is similar to a prison.

   It’s been protest after protest here since summer last
   year with both groups clashing, so today is nothing different for the community
   of Weymouth and Portland.

   Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
5. ### Crowds gather to protect Middlesbrough mosquepublished at 19:34 BST 4 August 202419:34 BST 4 August 2024

   ![Crowd gathers to guard a mosque](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/6a5100f2-9d13-43d3-8e03-66285fbc7f40.jpg.webp)Image source, Ian Forsyth/ Stringer

   Earlier today around 300 people gathered to guard the
   Jamia Masjid al-Madinah mosque in Middlesbrough, as anti-immigration demonstrations
   marched through the town.

   At
   least one car has been set alight in the Teesside town and burning wheelie bins
   have been pushed toward police officers.

   "We're going to be paying for this, and for
   what?" one resident told PA News Agency.

   "This has nothing to do with three little
   girls that have lost their lives. They've used it to cause utter mayhem on our
   streets".

   Cleveland Police say 14 arrests have been
   made so far.

   Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
6. ### Protesters contained in Weymouthpublished at 19:23 BST 4 August 202419:23 BST 4 August 2024

   **Sophie Cridland**  
   Reporting from Weymouth

   Police have
   contained protesters here on Weymouth esplanade.

   Around 30
   police from Dorset and the neighbouring counties of Devon and Cornwall are
   managing the groups.

   Both sides
   are still chanting with one shouting “save our streets” and the other “shame on
   you”

   Traffic is
   now moving and the buses and cars that were at a standstill have moved on with
   the road fully open around the King’s Statue.
   Police riot vans are still surrounding the pedestrianised sea front blocking in
   the protestors, away from members of the public.

   Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
7. ### Ten police officers injured in Rotherham hotel violencepublished at 19:12 BST 4 August 202419:12 BST 4 August 2024

   South Yorkshire Police (SYP) says at least 10 officers were injured in the "deplorable acts of violence" at the Holiday Inn Express Hotel in Rotherham earlier today.

   One officer was left unconscious after a head injury, SYP says, with other officers suffering suspected broken bones, including a suspected fractured elbow.

   There have so far been no reports of any hotel staff or residents being injured in the disorder.

   "We absolutely condemn the scenes of disorder we witnessed, with a total of around 700 people in attendance in Manvers Way earlier today," a statement says.

   "A large bin close to a window of the hotel was also set alight causing a small fire which was later extinguished, with missiles, including glass bottles and beer cans, thrown at our officers."

   ![Two masked men - one draped in an England flag - challenge police during violence outside a hotel in Rotherham.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/f5dcbe90-1e17-4ba0-8dec-94305953586f.jpg.webp)Image source, PAImage caption, 

   A crowd chanting "get them out" smashed windows at the Holiday Inn Express hotel.

   Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
8. ![Analysis](https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/core/website/assets/static/news/incident-types/analysis.77b314ef10.svg)

   ### Will Starmer's tough talk be enough to quell the unrest?published at 18:47 BST 4 August 202418:47 BST 4 August 2024

   ![](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/128/cpsprodpb//vivo/live/images/2024/3/7/8a0a1cd6-8a14-45af-b1e5-566c143fab9e.jpg.webp)**Hannah Miller**  
   Political correspondent

   Keir Starmer has sounded tough on the violent
   disorder for days - today branding it "far-right thuggery".

   His warning to those taking part that they will
   regret it extended not just to those committing criminal acts on the streets,
   but also those stirring it up online.

   Mosques have been offered emergency security, extra
   prosecutors have been brought in, and ministerial meetings have been held over
   the weekend.

   But still the violence continues, and it remains to
   be seen whether tough language will bring it to an end.

   The government believes that once people see
   arrests, charges and convictions becoming a reality, they will think twice
   about taking part.

   For
   communities and individuals who feel at risk, it has already been going on too
   long.

   Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
9. ### More police arrive in Weymouthpublished at 18:27 BST 4 August 202418:27 BST 4 August 2024

   **Sophie Cridland**  
   Reporting from Weymouth

   ![A line of riot police in Weymouth](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/2791ca4a-db67-40bc-86d6-908aad6261b0.jpg.webp)

   Dorset Police have built a physical wall using
   themselves and about five riot vans to stop the two protest groups meeting on
   the road and the esplanade.

   The anti-immigration group is slowly moving towards
   Stand up to Racism Dorset, which the police are trying to stop.

   They’ve called in more resources and in the last 10
   minutes, more police vans have turned up.

   Traffic
   is at a standstill in the town centre with buses and cars trying to leave the
   area, but they are stuck waiting at the King’s Statue.

   Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
10. ### Protesters face off in Weymouthpublished at 18:13 BST 4 August 202418:13 BST 4 August 2024

    **Sophie Cridland**  
    Reporting from Weymouth

    About 400 protestors from opposing sides are
    gathered on Weymouth seafront chanting at each other.

    On one side, the crowd is chanting "Weymouth
    and Portland welcome refugees" with placards saying "Nazis go
    home".

    The other group are chanting "Tommy
    Robinson", waving Union Jack flags and holding signs that read "I
    [love] my country".

    Officers have moved people off the beach because
    some anti-immigration protesters pushed through the police line and ran up the
    seafront.

    Protesters
    from opposing sides are gradually getting closer to each other.

    ![Police in a line on the beach, walking along](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/fc470400-f100-44df-840c-a190d6b5a6eb.png.webp)Image caption, 

    Police have asked people to leave Weymouth Beach

    Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
11. ### The latest on another day of clashespublished at 18:03 BST 4 August 202418:03 BST 4 August 2024

    ![A crowd of people watch on as a man fights with three police officers](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/d6bc7c9b-2d50-4be4-818b-ecc45d688bec.jpg.webp)Image source, PA Media

    There have been several fast-moving developments today - here's the latest:

    - In **Rotherham**, hundreds of anti-immigration protesters surrounded the Holiday Inn Express hotel, where some people lit a fire, smashed windows and doors and managed to get inside the building where asylum seekers were staying. Riot police surrounded the hotel and pushed crowds back, but the scene there remains tense
    - Crowds in **Middlesbrough** set fire to an overturned car and pushed burning wheelie bins at police
    - A protest in **Bolton** started off peacefully, but then became heated when two groups clashed. One, most with faces covered by face masks or balaclavas - charged towards town hall shouting "Allahu Akbar" - or God is greatest. They clashed with a separate group waving England flags
    - There were heated scenes in **Bolton** where two groups of protesters clashed
    - There have also been protests in **Hull** and **Weymouth**
    - PM Keir Starmer has condemned the **"far-right
      thuggery"** and vowed to do "whatever it takes to bring
      these thugs to justice"
    - The Home Office says that **mosques will be offered greater
      protection** with new "emergency security" measures that
      can be rapidly deployed where needed
    - And **147 arrests** have been made across the country since
      Saturday night, and police are expecting that number to rise

    Stay with us, we'll continue to bring you all the latest updates.

    Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
12. ### Rishi Sunak condemns 'criminal behaviour'published at 17:50 BST 4 August 202417:50 BST 4 August 2024

    ![Rishi sunak](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/aff7e0fb-84b2-4a7a-9d97-d366a944ef39.jpg.webp)Image source, Getty ImagesImage caption, 

    File photo

    Rishi Sunak says
    the unrest across the country is "violent, criminal behaviour that has no
    place in our society."

    The former
    PM, now leader of the opposition, [wrote on X, external](https://x.com/RishiSunak/status/1820132969513664691) that the violence has
    "nothing to do with the tragedy in Southport" and added that the
    police have "our full support to deal with these criminals swiftly".

    Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
13. ### 147 arrests and more to come, police chief sayspublished at 17:32 BST 4 August 202417:32 BST 4 August 2024

    Since Saturday night, police have made 147 arrests across the country, and that number is expected to rise, BJ Harrington of the National Police Chiefs’ Council has said [in a statement, external](https://news.npcc.police.uk/releases/public-order-lead-confirms-arrests-and-denounces-disinformation).

    He blamed disinformation for driving the "appalling violence", adding that "often posts are being shared and amplified by high profile accounts".

    He also had a warning for those involved in the unrest:

    “Intelligence teams, detectives and neighbourhood officers are working round the clock to identify and apprehend those involved and make no mistake, if you haven’t had a knock on the door yet, your time will come."

    Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
14. ### Car set alight and windows smashed as unrest continues in Middlesbroughpublished at 17:21 BST 4 August 202417:21 BST 4 August 2024

    ![A car burns after being overturned during an anti-immigration protest in Middlesbrough. Picture date: Sunday August 4, 2024](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/04f8f151-9ce4-490e-a1ae-6d7bb6fd4bd3.jpg.webp)Image source, PA Media

    We've seen crowds gather today in Rotherham, Bolton, Hull, Southport, Middlesbrough, and elsewhere, with varying levels of unrest.

    In Middlesbrough, the BBC team has witnessed demonstrators carry a banner referring to the Southport knife attacks, before throwing cans and bottles at police officers and shops with the shutters down.

    Youths wearing masks have attacked cars, with one overturned and set alight.

    Cars and homes in side streets around the town centre have also been targeted, their windows smashed. Wheelie bins have also been set alight.

    The local police force, Cleveland Police, say they've made nine arrests so far.

    People are being advised to stay away from the area.

    ![Police officers during an anti-immigration protest in Middlesbrough. Picture date: Sunday August 4, 2024. PA Photo](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/4b76bb0b-9bdb-416d-8e94-2fe1d5bb0ff4.jpg.webp)Image source, PA MediaShareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
15. ### Faith leaders say 'there is no place for hate'published at 17:13 BST 4 August 202417:13 BST 4 August 2024

    Faith leaders across Merseyside have said "too many people have sought to use the [Southport] tragedy to create division and hate".

    Leaders from the Catholic, Muslim, Jewish, Sikh and Hindu communities, among others, issued a joint statement this afternoon.

    "Division can destroy the very relationships and environment that we depend upon every day of our lives and there is no place for hate in our communities," it read.

    > Quote Message
    >
    > It can - and has - left communities in fear and has put people in danger.

    The statement called for "community spirit" and urged people to remember that "there is far more that unites than divides us".

    Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
16. ### Mosques will get new 'rapid response' security, Home Office sayspublished at 17:07 BST 4 August 202417:07 BST 4 August 2024Breaking

    The Home Office has just announced a new security scheme for mosques. It says mosques "at risk of violent disorder" will now be able to request "rapid response" personnel.

    "The police, local authorities and mosques can ask for rapid security to be deployed, protecting communities and allowing for a return to worship as quickly as possible," a Home Office statement says.

    The new security measures will "build on" the government's existing Protective Security for Mosques Scheme, which already funds security at mosques and Muslim schools.

    Home Secretary Yvette Cooper said: “As a nation we will not tolerate criminal behaviour, dangerous extremism, and racist attacks that go against everything our country stands for.”

    Earlier, PM Keir Starmer called unrest in UK streets "far-right thuggery", and said that Muslim communities had been targeted.

    Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
17. ### 'I know how frightening this must be'published at 16:54 BST 4 August 202416:54 BST 4 August 2024

    ![Keir Starmer](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2024/8/4/3737fba3-96da-49ef-8ecc-89570653b943.jpg.webp)Image source, Downing Street

    Starmer described the scenes in Rotherham as "marauding gangs intent on law-breaking and nothing else" and said they left "residents and staff in absolute fear".

    To those that feel targeted, he said: "I know how frightening it must be."

    He promised that the "violent mob do not represent our country" and said: "If you target people because of the colour of their skin or faith, that is far-right and I’m prepared to say so."

    "Our police deserve our support as they tackle any and all violent disorder that flares up," he added.

    > Quote Message
    >
    > Whatever the apparent cause or motive... we make no distinction: crime is crime, and this government will tackle it.

    The PM said he had held meetings throughout the weekend to make sure the "necessary support" is in place.

    Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
18. ### Starmer promises convictions to 'bring thugs to justice'published at 16:46 BST 4 August 202416:46 BST 4 August 2024

    Starmer vowed to do "whatever it takes to bring these thugs to justice" as he addressed the nation following ongoing unrest across parts of the country.

    He said there will be arrests, there will be charges and there will be convictions, "whatever the apparent cause or motivation".

    "This is not a protest, it is organised, violent thuggery and it has no place on our streets or online," he said.

    He specifically condemned an attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham this afternoon.

    The PM said those participating in violence, including those "whipping up this action online", would regret it.

    Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
19. ### PM condemns 'far-right thuggery'published at 16:39 BST 4 August 202416:39 BST 4 August 2024Breaking

    ## To play this video you need to enable JavaScript in your browser.

    This video can not be played

    Keir Starmer has condemned what he has called "far-right thuggery" in the form of violent demonstrations.

    Speaking to reporters at Downing Street just now, the prime minister said those involved will feel the "full force of the law".

    "You will regret taking part in this," he told those involved in the unrest.

    "There is no justification for taking this action."

    He said people in this country "have a right to be safe" and yet we've seen "Muslim communities targeted... Nazi salutes in the street".

    "I won't shy away from calling it what it is - far-right thuggery," Starmer said.

    Shareclose panelShare pageCopy link[About sharing](https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms/can-i-share-things-from-the-bbc)
20. ### Rotherham violence 'utterly appalling' says Cooperpublished at 16:31 BST 4 August 202416:31 BST 4 August 2024Breaking

    Home Secretary Yvette Cooper has posted on X about the scenes in Rotherham.

    "The criminal, violent attack on a hotel housing asylum seekers in Rotherham is utterly appalling," she says.

    "Deliberately setting fire to a building with people known to be inside.
    South Yorkshire Police have full Government support for the strongest action against those responsible."
